title: Manuel Carlevaro
text:
After finishing high school in Paraná, I began my university studies, simultaneously pursuing two degrees: Electromechanical Engineering at the Universidad Tecnológica Nacional, and Bioengineering at the Universidad Nacional de Entre Ríos. But I really wanted to study physics, so in 1990 I moved to the city of Rosario.
I graduated with a Licentiate in Physics from the Universidad Nacional de Rosario in 1995, and then obtained my PhD from the Facultad de Ciencias Exactas of the Universidad Nacional de La Plata in 2002, thanks to a doctoral fellowship from CONICET.



After completing my PhD, I worked as a researcher in the Agribusiness and Food Program of the Universidad de Buenos Aires (2001 – 2003), developing computational models of agrifood chains. Subsequently, I worked at the Nuclear Regulatory Authority (2003 – 2007) on projects related to the Comprehensive Nuclear-Test-Ban Treaty.
In 2007, I joined CONICET as an Assistant Researcher at the Instituto de Física de Líquidos y Sistemas Biológicos (IFLySiB), which depends on CONICET and the Universidad Nacional de La Plata, where I currently work, but now as an Independent Researcher.
As a teacher, I worked between 1993 and 1996 at the Universidad Nacional de Rosario (in the faculties of Engineering, Medicine, Veterinary Medicine, and Biochemistry and Pharmacy), and also at the Facultad de Ciencias Exactas of the Universidad Nacional de La Plata between 1997 and 2001. In 2005, I started as an Adjunct Professor at the Facultad Regional Buenos Aires of the Universidad Tecnológica Nacional, teaching Physics II, and since 2018 I have been working as a Full Professor at the Facultad Regional La Plata of the same university, teaching undergraduate courses (currently Advanced Calculus) as well as graduate courses.

title: C. Manuel Carlevaro
text:
I am a physicist with diverse research interests in the fields of biophysics, granular matter physics, confined fluids, and complex systems.
I am currently an Independent Researcher at the Instituto de Física de Líquidos y Sistemas Biológicos which depends on CONICET and the Universidad Nacional de La Plata.
I am also a Full Professor at the Facultad Regional La Plata of the Universidad Tecnológica Nacional, where I direct the Centro de Investigación en Mecánica Experimental y Computacional and teach the Advanced Calculus course in the Mechanical Engineering Department.

title: C. Manuel Carlevaro
text:
Education

Ph.D. in Physics, 2002.
Universidad Nacional de La Plata, La Plata, Argentina.
Licenciado in Physics, 1995. Universidad Nacional de Rosario, Rosario, Argentina.
Bachelor in Biological Sciences, 1985. Escuela Normal Superior José María Torres, Paraná, Argentina.

title: C. Manuel Carlevaro
text:

Computational modeling and simulation

Biophysics
Complex systems

title: C. Manuel Carlevaro
text:
Book
I am the co-author, along with Facundo Batista, of «Python para Ciencia y Tecnología». It is an independently published book in Spanish that covers basic, intermediate, and advanced aspects of the Python programming language, as well as some applications in science and technology. It is distributed for free in PDF format.


Sponsors
Institutions and organizations that support my work:

title: Manuel Carlevaro
text:
Graduate Courses

Introduction to Granular Materials Physics. Graduate course valid for the Ph.D. in Engineering, Materials mention, Universidad Tecnológica Nacional. Coordinators: Dr. Manuel Carlevaro and Dr. Luis Pugnaloni. Duration: 100 hours (2025).
Computational Tools for Scientists (HCC). Graduate course valid for the Ph.D. programs of the Facultad de Ciencias Exactas and Facultad de Ciencias Astronómicas y Geofísicas at Universidad Nacional de La Plata, and for the Ph.D. in Engineering at Universidad Tecnológica Nacional. Coordinators: Dr. Manuel Carlevaro and Dr. Ramiro Irastorza (since 2018, previously Dr. Luis Pugnaloni). Duration: 70 hours (2007 – 2024).
Physics and Applications of Granular Matter. New Jersey Institute of Technology, Department of Mathematical Sciences and Ministry of Education of Argentina. Coordinator: Lou Kondic (Distinguished Professor NJIT). Duration: 140 hours (2002).
Computational Tools for Statistical Mechanics. Universidad Nacional de La Pampa, Facultad de Ciencias Exactas y Naturales. Responsible professors: Dr. Luis Pugnaloni, Dr. Manuel Carlevaro. Duration: 20 hours (2017).
Liquids and Disordered Systems. Universidad Nacional de San Luis, Facultad de Ciencias Físico Matemáticas y Naturales. Responsible professors: Dr. Tomás Grigera and Dr. Manuel Carlevaro. Duration: 20 hours (2015).
Introduction to Dynamical Systems and Chaos Theory. Graduate and Continuing Education School. Facultad de Ingeniería, Universidad Nacional de La Plata. Coordinator: Dr. Fernando Vericat. Duration: 60 hours (2007, 2010).
Introduction to Programming, Numerical Calculus, and Simulation for Scientists. Instituto de Física de Líquidos y Sistemas Biológicos. Facultad de Ciencias Exactas, Universidad Nacional de La Plata. Coordinators: Dr. Luis Pugnaloni, Dr. Manuel Carlevaro. Duration: 70 hours (2007).
